author: analyst
title: n(r) discrepancy reconciliation: formula coefficient changed from 0.27 to 0.20

analysis.source: WebFetch of john09289.github.io/predictions (2026-04-10), interactive calculator + Python code block

our_review_states.Part 7 disc edge: n reaching 28.8 (DOUBLE ERROR: disc edge is ice wall at r=20,015, not r=40,000; and even r=40,000 gives 21.53)

analysis.coefficient_hypothesis: With coefficient 0.27 instead of 0.20: n(20015)=3.48, n(40000)=28.71. This matches our Part 1 values (3.49, 28.8). BUT n(14105)=2.12, not 1.40. Our equatorial ring value does not match ANY coefficient.
analysis.likely_explanation: Dome changed coefficient from ~0.27 to 0.20. Our review was written against older coefficient. The 1.40 value at equatorial ring may be from yet another formula version.
analysis.poller_observation: Poller says dome page shows 3.49 at ice wall. This is inconsistent with 0.20 coefficient extracted via WebFetch. Possible: interactive calculator uses different coefficient than displayed formula, or dome updated between poller run and this fetch.

errors_in_our_review: Part 7: 28.8 at disc edge -- disc edge is ice wall (r=20,015), not r=40,000. At ice wall n is 2.84 (current) or 3.49 (old coeff). 28.8 only at r=40,000 which is beyond dome geometry.
Part 1: Equatorial ring n=1.40 does not match ANY version of dome formula tested.
Part 1: Ice wall n=3.49 and r=40,000 n=28.8 appear to use old coefficient 0.27, not current 0.20.
recommendation: Correct as part of CAT-005 Refraction Cascade work (EXP-050). Fixes: (1) Recompute all n(r) values using current dome formula (coeff 0.20). (2) Fix Part 7 'disc edge' to cite correct radius and value. (3) Note dome WEAKENED refraction claim by lowering coefficient -- makes our critique stronger. (4) Add computation table for transparency.
blocked_by: ISS-696 (curmudgeon review of CAT-005/EXP-050). This research note provides data for that resolution.
kernel_of_truth: The dome has a well-defined computable refraction formula (not hand-waved). Monotonically increasing with radius, n=1 at pole. Coefficient reduction from ~0.27 to 0.20 suggests genuine iterative refinement. However even at 0.20, n=2.84 at ice wall exceeds diamond (n=2.42) and the formula is unfalsifiable since no measurement of aetheric refraction is possible.
